lede: A look at July 26 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Thomasin McKenzie and Stephen Makinwa, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hillary Clinton
events.desc: Hillary Clinton becomes the first female nominee for president of the United States by a major political party at the Democratic National Convention in Philadelphia.

events.title: Sagamihara stabbings
events.desc: The Sagamihara stabbings occur in Kanagawa Prefecture in Japan. Nineteen people are killed.

events.title: Asiana Airlines Flight 733
events.desc: Asiana Airlines Flight 733 crashes into a ridge on Mt. Ungeo on its third attempt to land at Mokpo Airport, South Korea. Sixty-eight of the 116 people on board are killed.

events.title: Quebec
events.desc: The National Assembly of Quebec imposes the use of French as the official language of the provincial government.

events.title: Fidel Castro
events.desc: Cold War: Fidel Castro leads an unsuccessful attack on the Moncada Barracks, thus beginning the Cuban Revolution. The movement took the name of the date: 26th of July Movement

events.title: HMS Vestal (J215)
events.desc: World War II: HMS Vestal is the last British Royal Navy ship to be sunk in the war.

events.title: Ulises Heureaux
events.desc: Ulises Heureaux, the 27th President of the Dominican Republic, is assassinated.

events.title: University of Wisconsin–Madison
events.desc: The University of Wisconsin–Madison is established by Nelson Dewey, first Governor of Wisconsin.

events.title: French and Indian War
events.desc: French and Indian War: The Siege of Louisbourg ends with British forces defeating the French and taking control of the Gulf of Saint Lawrence.

events.title: Bavarian Rummel
events.desc: During the Bavarian Rummel the rural population of Tyrol drove the Bavarian Prince-Elector Maximilian II Emanuel out of North Tyrol with a victory at the Pontlatzer Bridge and thus prevented the Bavarian Army, which was allied with France, from marching as planned on Vienna during the War of the Spanish Succession.

births.name: Jesse Livermore
births.line: American investor and security analyst, "Great Bear of Wall Street" (died 1940)

deaths.text: Tom Lehrer, American singer, comedian and mathematician
George W. Romney, American businessman and politician, 43rd Governor of Michigan
Thomas Osborne, 1st Duke of Leeds, English politician, Lord High Treasurer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Andrew of Phú Yên
Christian feast day: Paraskevi of Rome (Eastern Orthodox Church)
